title: Engineering & Administrative Controls
description: Practical implementation of laser safety controls: barriers, interlocks, PPE, and standard operating procedures.

phases.title: Hierarchy of Controls
phases.content: Not all controls are equal. The hierarchy of controls prioritizes engineering solutions over administrative procedures and personal protective equipment. Understanding this hierarchy ensures resources are allocated to the most effective safety measures.

phases.title: Implementing Laser Safety Controls
phases.content: Effective laser safety relies on a combination of engineering controls, administrative procedures, and personal protective equipment. Engineering controls like enclosures, interlocks, and beam stops are most effective because they do not rely on human behavior. Administrative controls such as SOPs and training are secondary. PPE including laser protective eyewear is the last line of defense and must be properly selected for wavelength and optical density requirements.

phases.title: Control Selection Exercise
phases.content:
For each scenario, identify appropriate controls using the hierarchy:

**Scenario 1**: Class 4 laser in a research laboratory

**Scenario 2**: Laser light show in an entertainment venue

**Scenario 3**: Industrial laser cutting operation

**Scenario 4**: Medical laser procedure room

keyTakeaways: Engineering controls (enclosures, interlocks) are preferred over administrative controls
Laser Controlled Areas must be clearly posted with warning signs
Personal Protective Equipment (OD-rated eyewear) is the last line of defense
Written SOPs must be developed and followed for all Class 3B/4 operations
